title: He Solved 2 Rubik's Cubes While Skydiving at 120 MPH
script: Mid-air Rubik's cube record set! On June 11, Ishaan Hadkar, 24, set a Guinness World Record for the most rotating puzzle cubes solved during a single skydive — and it was only his first time ever skydiving. At 13,000 feet over Oceanside, California, he fell at 120 miles per hour. On his first attempt, one of the cubes actually broke mid-air. He tried again. This time, Hadkar solved the first cube in freefall and the second under the parachute — 2 total. The weather was windy and cloudy. None of that stopped him. He'd been solving Rubik's cubes since he was 10. Now he holds the world record. How many Rubik's cubes can you solve at 120 miles per hour?
